name: Prabhacandra
dates_notes: Prabhācandra is conventionally placed in the late tenth to mid-eleventh century from Jaina scholastic succession and from the works he comments on and extends.
core_thesis: Prabhācandra is a Digambara Jaina logician whose major works continue the Akalaṅka-Māṇikyanandi line of Jaina pramāṇa theory. The *Prameya-Kamala-Mārtaṇḍa* comments on Māṇikyanandi's *Parīkṣāmukha* and expands its concise account of valid cognition, inference, standpoints, and Jaina realism into a large scholastic commentary. The *Nyāya-Kumuda-Candra* is a sustained Jaina work of logic and epistemology, engaging Buddhist pramāṇa theory and Brahmanical Nyāya from the standpoint of Jaina anekānta, naya analysis, and the substance-mode account of real things. The entry treats his doctrine only at this established level: Prabhācandra is a commentator and systematizer of Digambara Jaina logic, not an independent source for claims not grounded in those named works.

engaged_works.title: Prameya-Kamala-Martanda
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Standard Digambara Jaina and modern scholarly references ascribe the commentary to Prabhācandra.
engaged_works.summary: A large commentary on Māṇikyanandi's *Parīkṣāmukha*, explaining the Jaina account of valid cognition and the categories needed for inference and debate. The work receives the compact sūtra-style base text and develops its positions in the post-Akalaṅka scholastic idiom: cognition is assessed through the Jaina pramāṇa framework, predication is conditioned by standpoint, and real things are treated as substance-mode complexes rather than as either momentary particulars or unqualified substances. Its importance for this roster is mainly genealogical and dialectical: it shows how the shorter Jaina logic manual became a fully developed commentary tradition in conversation with Buddhist and Naiyāyika opponents.

engaged_works.title: Nyaya-Kumuda-Candra
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Standard Jaina bibliographies and modern accounts of Jaina logic treat the work as Prabhācandra's.
engaged_works.summary: An extensive Jaina treatise in logic and epistemology. The work belongs to the same scholastic environment as the *Prameya-Kamala-Mārtaṇḍa* and develops Jaina replies to rival accounts of cognition, inference, universals, and ontological determination. Its Jaina position is the mature Digambara dialectical one: cognition and predication must be handled through a many-sided account of real objects, while one-sided Buddhist and Naiyāyika analyses are rejected where they cannot account for the persistence and modal variation of things. The surviving printed editions are scan-level resources rather than a clean corpus text here.
